title: Zingy & Fresh Creamy Courgette Carbonara
summary: This might be an untraditional take on a carbonara but it's seriously good. It's the definition of summer comfort. We got all the ingredients in as little as 20 minutes from Deliveroo.

method.description: Place a saucepan of salted water on to the boil. Once boiling, cook the pasta until it's 1 min under al dente.
Trim and shave the courgette with a peeler until you get to the middle then thinly slice the centres. Finely slice the garlic.
Crack a whole egg into a bowl, add the separate yolks and whisk. Finely grate the Parmesan into the eggs and mix together with the chilli flakes.
Add the butter into a frying pan, place onto medium heat and let it melt. Stir fry the courgette centres and garlic, season with salt and cook until softened, 5-7 mins.

Using tongs, transfer the pasta straight from the water into the garlic with the courgette. Turn the heat up to medium-high, toss together until the courgette has just started to wilt, then turn the heat down to low. Add a splash of pasta water and the egg mixture. Toss until glossy and thickened. Zest half the lemon in, season with a squeeze of lemon juice and more salt. Add a splash of pasta water to loosen, if needed.
Share the pasta between bowls and top with more Parmesan.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
